file_insights.purpose: Django ORM models for menu application

key_components.name: LogoPosition
key_components.description: Enumeration for logo positioning (LEFT, CENTER, RIGHT)

key_components.name: Theme
key_components.description: Menu styling customization model with color, font, and CSS options

patterns_discovered.evidence: Every query comment emphasizes filtering by organization, ForeignKey relationship, and unique_together constraint
patterns_discovered.relevance: Critical for data isolation in SaaS architecture

patterns_discovered.evidence: SoftDeleteMixin usage, soft_delete() method documentation, all_objects manager alongside objects manager
patterns_discovered.relevance: Preserves data integrity and audit trails

patterns_discovered.evidence: Extensive docstrings with Usage examples, Critical Rules, and attribute descriptions
patterns_discovered.relevance: Enterprise-ready code with clear API contracts

patterns_discovered.evidence: SoftDeleteManager for default queries, all_objects manager for complete access
patterns_discovered.relevance: Prevents accidental queries on deleted records
Global uniqueness and distributed system readiness
Encapsulates business logic and reduces code duplication

patterns_discovered.evidence: Multiple indexes on organization, is_active, is_default, and deleted_at
patterns_discovered.relevance: Query optimization for common filtering patterns

gotchas_discovered.gotcha: Color field max_length=7 assumes hex format
gotchas_discovered.issue: No validation that values are valid hex colors (e.g., #RRGGBB)
gotchas_discovered.recommendation: Consider adding regex validator or custom field validation

gotchas_discovered.issue: Could leave multiple defaults if race condition occurs
gotchas_discovered.recommendation: Wrap in transaction.atomic() and use select_for_update()

gotchas_discovered.issue: Django best practice is default=dict (callable), but documentation shows it correctly
gotchas_discovered.note: Actually correctly implemented - default=dict is callable

gotchas_discovered.gotcha: Only one theme per organization should have is_default=True
gotchas_discovered.issue: No constraint enforcement at database level (unique constraint missing)
gotchas_discovered.recommendation: Add conditional unique constraint or database trigger

gotchas_discovered.gotcha: custom_css field allows arbitrary CSS injection
gotchas_discovered.issue: Potential security risk if exposed to frontend without sanitization
gotchas_discovered.recommendation: Add documentation warning about sanitization requirements

gotchas_discovered.gotcha: Font family field accepts any string without validation
gotchas_discovered.issue: No validation that specified fonts are available/safe
gotchas_discovered.recommendation: Consider whitelist or Google Fonts integration

approach_outcome.deliverable: Complete Theme model with enterprise-ready features
features_delivered: Color customization (primary, secondary, background, text, accent)
approach_outcome.code_organization: Single well-structured file with clear imports, enumerations, and model definition

recommendations.details: Implement validators.RegexValidator for color fields to ensure valid hex format (#[0-9A-Fa-f]{6})
Add database-level constraint: ALTER TABLE themes ADD CONSTRAINT unique_default_per_org UNIQUE (organization_id, is_default) WHERE is_default = true
Use @transaction.atomic() decorator and select_for_update() to prevent race conditions
Add security warning in custom_css field help_text about sanitization requirements for frontend usage
Test soft delete behavior, multi-tenant isolation, CSS variable generation, set_as_default logic, and default values
Document how to handle theme migration when organizations are created/updated
Add ability to create themes from predefined templates (light, dark, high-contrast) for faster setup
Cache get_css_variables() output in Redis with cache invalidation on model save
